Sirentide — Tensor-network diagram

Live renderer output — the SVG below was produced by Sirentide from the DSL beside it.

tensornetwork
mps A B C D

A tensor-network diagram in Penrose graphical notation — the notation of quantum-information / DMRG / tensor-network-ML papers. mps A B C D lays out a matrix-product STATE: an ordered row of tensor cores (discs), a bond (the contracted virtual index) drawn between every adjacent pair, and one dangling physical leg per core. mpo A B C D makes it a matrix-product OPERATOR — each core gains a second vertical leg (one physical index up, one down). Each core anchors as a node and each bond as an edge, so the chain is queryable and plays through frame-by-frame like every other type; pure line/disc geometry, no runtime dependency.

All sixteen types on one page: showcase.html · browser-audited renders: gallery
